reviews.review_description:
This app should not be behind a subscription paywall in its current condition—plain and simple.

Charging $10/month (or ~$100/year) for a simulator that barely functions is unacceptable. This is not a live service product. It feels like an unfinished prototype being sold as a premium experience.

The biggest issue is performance. The app is full of bugs, and even the tutorial doesn’t work properly. If users can’t complete the tutorial or interact with basic controls, the app fails at its core purpose.

The value just isn’t there. Firefighters already have access to real-world training and equipment. For a VR alternative to make sense, it needs to be polished, reliable, and realistic. This isn’t.

Right now, this feels like a cash grab—not a serious training tool.

Unless major improvements are made and the pricing model is reconsidered, I would not recommend this app to anyone.

This is the best firefighting game I’ve ever played. It tought me a lot about firefighting and about the truck. The game pass makes it even more fun with challenges, missions and even partners. This is the best game ever!
Bro, you can’t even open the doors in the game. You can’t even get the hose out to spray the fire.
Even the tutorial is full of bugs. Can't touch anything to even run through the tutorial. Worthless.

Was looking to use this for training for EVOC but instead I’m met with a unusable game. The tutorial runs through how to use a pump very basically and afterwards you get nothing. You can’t set up a supply line without a subscription, you can’t pump unless in the tutorial or buying a subscription for freeplay. Big waste of time, back to the books.

Love the idea but, just to hard to press stuff. Tutorials get stuck. And overall it makes me short in the simulator. I am 5"10, and it makes me 4"1.

Either way love it, great training for firefighters.

developer_response.body: Thanks for reviewing Pumpers!  We would love to get more details (on where you get stuck) via our website "contact us" form.  FYI, we have no control over the floor, please use the Meta roomscale/stationary boundary adjustments to correct it.

reviews.review_description:
Really, A subscription based app? Who in their right mind would want to have a "game" and pay $100/year for the privilege to play it.  This seems like a "single purchase" type of app. I will be honest, I have not tried it. I downloaded it, then saw the $10/month price tag and was immediately looking to be rid of it. The only places that would conceivably pay the subscription fee are actual firehouses, and they have ACTUAL PUMPS that they can practice on. And, many of them are looking to get the real pumps out to cycle anyway. This is a scam.

Edit: they have asked for this review to be removed as "defamatory". I have admitted to not playing the game.  But the idea of charging people $100/year for access to a game is ridiculous.  Sounds like they may be embarrassed by the business model. I was going to ask my firehouse if this was something they would be interested in, but the ongoing cost is untenable.
Didn’t know how to get the tools since it all costs money

reviews.review_title: Great but needs more due to subscription
reviews.review_description: From what I was able to see it's a great start and informative for training but I think charging a subscription price should not be a thing until the "game/sim" is out of beta.  It would be cool to see portable pumps added aswell as other truck models with less tech and or more tech. The controls also need work.

developer_response.body: Thank you for your valuable feedback. We appreciate your suggestions and are continuously developing improvements, including new features and better controls.  If you have specific details to share with us, feel free to use our contacts page (website) so we can make Pumpers even better.  Stay tuned for more exciting updates!

reviews.review_description: Looks hella realistic and you can refresh your pump skills in your living room

As someone who has always admired the courage and dedication of firefighters,ever since I was a little kid I always wanted to be a firefighter now at 19 I am a volunteer firefighter possibility of becoming a career firefighter further on. I was thrilled to dive into the world of VR firefighter training games. After spending several hours with one such simulation, I must say that the experience exceeded my expectations in terms of realism, educational value, and sheer adrenaline.
A very immersive pump ops experience with great walkthroughs. Freestyle mode does what I need for training our junior operators effectively.
